steps.title: Find DC using the 30-60-90 triangle BDC
steps.content:
Triangle BDC has a right angle at D (BD ⊥ AC), angle BCD = 60°, and angle DBC = 30°. This is a 30-60-90 right triangle.

In a 30-60-90 triangle, the sides opposite 30°, 60°, and 90° are in the ratio $1 : \sqrt{3} : 2$.

With BC = 12 (the hypotenuse), we have:
$$DC = 12 \cdot \frac{1}{2} = 6$$
$$BD = 12 \cdot \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} = 6\sqrt{3}$$

steps.title: Find AD using the 30-60-90 triangle ABD
steps.content:
Triangle ABD has a right angle at D, angle ABD = 30°, and angle BAD = 60°. This is also a 30-60-90 triangle.

Using the same side ratio, with BD = $6\sqrt{3}$ (the side opposite 60°):
$$AD = \frac{6\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{3}} = 6$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
